intended_use: ME-APDSTM (Magentiq Eye's Automatic Polyp Detection System) is intended to be used by endoscopists as an adjunct to the common video colonoscopy procedure (screening and surveillance), aiming to assis
intended_use_full: ME-APDSTM (Magentiq Eye's Automatic Polyp Detection System) is intended to be used by endoscopists as an adjunct to the common video colonoscopy procedure (screening and surveillance), aiming to assist the endoscopist in identifying lesions during colonoscopy procedure by highlighting regions with visual characteristics consistent with different types of mucosal abnormalities that appear in the colonoscopy video during the procedure.
ground_truth: Histopathological findings (344 polyps had histology; 277 were adenoma)
other_metrics: Polyp-wise Recall (PRecall1-PRecall7): 99.7% to 99.7% for polyps with histology, 97.9% to 90.6% for all polyps; Frame-based Recall (FRecall): 73.1% [70.56%, 75.75%]; Mean Polyp Coverage (MPC): 72%; False Positives Per Frame (FPPF): 0.0333; Marker annotation latency: 133 msec (FHD), 157 msec (4K)
